heading: Codification of sanctions with respect to North Korean activities undermining cybersecurity
label: 9230 Codification of sanctions with respect to North Korean activities undermining cybersecurity

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: United States sanctions with respect to activities of the Government of North Korea, persons acting for or on behalf of that Government, or persons located in North Korea that undermine cybersecurity provided for in Executive Order 13687 ( note; relating to imposing additional sanctions with respect to North Korea) or Executive Order 13694 ( note; relating to blocking the property of certain persons engaging in significant malicious cyber-enabled activities), as such Executive Orders are in effect on the day before , shall remain in effect until the date that is 30 days after the date on which the President submits to Congress a certification that the Government of North Korea, persons acting for or on behalf of that Government, and persons owned or controlled, directly or indirectly, by that Government or persons acting for or on behalf of that Government, are no longer engaged in the illicit activities described in such Executive Orders, including actions in violation of United Nations Security Council Resolutions 1718 (2006), 1874 (2009), 2087 (2013), and 2094 (2013).50 U.S.C. 170150 U.S.C. 1701February 18, 2016

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: b Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the authority of the President pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 1701
